title: RealMLP PyTabKit original-kernel baseline
summary: Port the high-performing public RealMLP/PyTabKit notebook into a reusable legacy root hypothesis for further branch exploration.
rationale: The public kernel combines competition train/test with the original F1 strategy dataset, applies compact interaction and categorical encodings, then trains RealMLP with fold-wise original-data augmentation while validating only on competition rows. This produced the strongest observed local and public score among the tested variants.
implementation_hint: Use the included legacy code as the root implementation. Keep the original dataset as a separate input file named f1_strategy_dataset_v4.csv, drop Normalized_TyreLife from it, append only each fold's original-training split to the competition-training split, and keep validation on competition rows only.
expected_effect: Establish a strong neural tabular root for exploring incremental branch hypotheses beyond the AutoGluon roots.
risk: The approach depends on pytabkit/torch availability and may be slower or weaker without a working GPU; the public-kernel validation protocol may not be directly comparable to all previous AIDE-generated roots.
